title: Ra Ra Ramaiya Lyrics Baasha
stanzas:
Ra Ra Ramaiya Song Lyrics Baasha
#SPB #Deva #Vairamuthu

Orey oru Chandhiranthaan
iravukellam.
orey oru sooriyanthaan
pagazhukellaam

orey oru baashaa thaan
indha oorukellaam(3)

Ra Ra ramaiya
ettu kulla ulagam
irukku ramaiyaa

ada Ra Ra ramaiya
naan puttu puttu
vaikkaporen paraiyaa

ettu kulla vazhka
irukku ramiya(2)
buthikku ettumpadi
sollaporen kelaiyaa

ikkada Ra Ra ramaiya
ettu kulla ulagam
irukku ramaiyaa

ada Ra Ra ramaiya
naan puttu puttu
vaikkaporen paraiyaa

mudhal ettil aadaathadhu
vilaiyaatalla.
nee rendaam ettil
kallaaladhu kalviyum alla(2)

moondraa ettil seiyaathadhu
thirumanam alla.
nee naalaam ettil perathadhu
kuzhandhaiyum alla

ettu ettaa manushan
vazhva prichiko(2)
nee endha ettil
ipppa irukka nenachiko

Ra Ra ramaiya
ettu kulla ulagam
irukku ramaiyaa

ada Ra Ra ramaiya
naan puttu puttu
vaikkaporen paraiyaa

aindhaam ettil serkaathadhu
selvamum alla.
nee aaram ettil sutraathadhu
ulagamum alla.(2)

ezhaam ettil kaanaathadhu
oivum illa.
nee ettaam ettukku maela
irundha nimmadhi illa

ettu ettaa manushan
vazhva prichiko(2)
nee endha ettil
ipppa irukka nenachiko

Ra Ra ramaiya
ettu kulla ulagam
irukku ramaiyaa

ada Ra Ra ramaiya
naan puttu puttu
vaikkaporen paraiyaa

ettu kulla vazhka
irukku ramiya(2)
buthikku ettumpadi
sollaporen kelaiyaa

Ra Ra ramaiya
ettu kulla ulagam
irukku ramaiyaa

ada Ra Ra ramaiya
naan puttu puttu
vaikkaporen paraiyaa

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song 'Ra Ra Ramaiya'?

The song celebrates the essence of life and joy, reflecting the vibrant spirit of the character in the movie.

What is the plot context of the movie Baasha?

Baasha revolves around a man with a mysterious past who becomes a protector of his community, and this song highlights his energetic persona.

Who is the singer and music director of this song?

The song is sung by the legendary S. P. Balasubrahmanyam and composed by Deva.

When was 'Ra Ra Ramaiya' released?

The song was released on January 12, 1995.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Ra Ra Ramaiya, the exuberance of life is captured through lively beats and engaging lyrics. The song serves as a celebration of the protagonist's vibrant personality, showcasing his connection with the community. It evokes feelings of joy and nostalgia, making it a perfect anthem for festivities and gatherings. The powerful vocals of S. P. Balasubrahmanyam combined with Deva's energetic composition create an unforgettable musical experience.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Ra Ra Ramaiya from Baasha is a vibrant celebration of life and energy, perfectly encapsulating the spirit of the film. With the legendary voice of S. P. Balasubrahmanyam and the masterful composition by Deva, this track is sure to get you moving. Experience the nostalgia and joy that this classic brings to every gathering!
stanzaMeanings: There is only one moon for the night. There is only one sun for the day.
There is only one king for this town.
Ra Ra Ramaiya, there is a world within eight boundaries.
Hey Ra Ra Ramaiya, I will keep putting it in pieces, listen.
Life exists within eight boundaries, but I will tell you about the end of life, listen.
Here too, Ra Ra Ramaiya, there is a world within eight boundaries.
Hey Ra Ra Ramaiya, I will keep putting it in pieces, listen.
In the first place, not everything is a game. You are not a thief in the second place either.
In the third place, not everything is a marriage. You are not a child in the fourth place either.
Life exists within eight boundaries, think about which place you are in now.
Ra Ra Ramaiya, there is a world within eight boundaries.
Hey Ra Ra Ramaiya, I will keep putting it in pieces, listen.
In the fifth place, not everything is wealth. You are not the world that wanders in the sixth place either.
In the seventh place, not everything is a solution. You are not at peace if you are above the eighth place.
Life exists within eight boundaries, think about which place you are in now.
Ra Ra Ramaiya, there is a world within eight boundaries.
Hey Ra Ra Ramaiya, I will keep putting it in pieces, listen.
Life exists within eight boundaries, but I will tell you about the end of life, listen.
Ra Ra Ramaiya, there is a world within eight boundaries.
Hey Ra Ra Ramaiya, I will keep putting it in pieces, listen.
